when_to_use: To say you do NOT do something, or someone does NOT do something.
when_to_use_ar: لمّا تبي تقول إنك ما تسوي شي، أو شخص ما يسوي شي.

the_rule.i_you_we_they: don't + base verb → I don't like. We don't have. They don't know.
the_rule.he_she_it: doesn't + base verb → He doesn't work. She doesn't speak. It doesn't matter.
the_rule.critical: After don't/doesn't, the verb is ALWAYS base form. Never add -s after doesn't.

common_errors.explanation: English uses 'don't' — not just 'no'. 'No' is only for nouns: 'no money', 'no time'.
After doesn't, NEVER add -s. The -s is already in 'does'.
Use 'am not' only with adjectives (I'm not happy). For verbs, use don't/doesn't.

pronunciation_note.doesnt: doesn't = /DUZ-uhnt/ — sounds like 'DUZent' (schwa in the second syllable)
pronunciation_note.tip: In fast speech: 'I don't know' → 'I dunno' /ah DUH-noh/

short_answers: Do you like coffee? → No, I don't.
Does she work here? → No, she doesn't.
also_useful.contractions: do not = don't · does not = doesn't (always use the short form in speaking)

quick_rule: I/you/we/they + DON'T + verb. He/she/it + DOESN'T + verb. After don't/doesn't = base form ALWAYS.
quick_rule_ar: أنا/أنت/نحن/هم + DON'T + فعل. هو/هي + DOESN'T + فعل. بعد don't/doesn't = الشكل الأساسي دائماً.
connect_to_speaking: Practice: 'I don't work on weekends. I don't eat fish. My brother doesn't live here. She doesn't speak English.' Natural negation is essential for real conversation.
